Test Scenario: Personalised greeting renders for signed-in user
Pre-conditions: User signed-in to Power Apps Player with valid Entra ID; GraphicTrackerUser role assigned
Test Steps: 1. Launch app via apps.powerapps.com/play/{appId}; 2. Wait for scr_Landing to load; 3. Observe lblHello header
Expected Result: lblHello reads "Hello Kuldeep Garud" populated from gblUser.displayName (Office365Users.MyProfileV2)

Test Scenario: Three navigation buttons render and navigate correctly
Pre-conditions: App opens without error on scr_Landing
Test Steps: 1. Click "Create a New Graphic Tracker"; 2. Back(); 3. Click "Add/Update requests in existing Graphics Tracker"; 4. Back(); 5. Click "Open Existing Graphic Tracker"
Expected Result: Buttons navigate to scr_CreateTracker, scr_ViewTracker (active channel context), and scr_OpenTracker respectively with ScreenTransition.None

Test Scenario: SharePoint folder & naming convention link opens in new tab
Pre-conditions: Dataverse env variable gt_SharePointGuideUrl is configured
Test Steps: 1. Tap lnkSharePoint on scr_Landing; 2. Observe browser tab
Expected Result: Launch() opens the configured SharePoint URL in a new browser tab

Test Scenario: App.OnStart initialises gblUser and gblTheme within 1s
Test Steps: 1. Launch app; 2. In Power Apps Studio Monitor inspect App.OnStart timing; 3. Inspect gblUser and gblTheme
Expected Result: gblUser populated from Office365Users.MyProfileV2(); gblTheme contains Accenture primary RGBA(161,0,255,1); OnStart completes within 1s

Test Scenario: Entra displayName missing falls back gracefully
Pre-conditions: Test account exists with no displayName populated in Entra ID
Test Steps: 1. Sign-in as account without displayName; 2. Open scr_Landing
Expected Result: lblHello shows "Hello there" or UPN prefix; app does not crash

Test Scenario: Create tracker with valid 6-digit OppId and unique values
Pre-conditions: colMyTeams populated; OppId 123456 not present in gt_graphictracker
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to scr_CreateTracker; 2. Enter OppId 123456; 3. Enter Deal Name; 4. Select Team; 5. Select Channel; 6. Tap btnCreate
Test Data: OppId: 123456; Deal Name: Acme RFP; Team: PursuitOps; Channel: Deal-123456
Expected Result: Patch writes one row to gt_graphictracker; app navigates to scr_ViewTracker with gblActiveTracker set; alt-key Unique(gt_opportunityid) satisfied

Test Scenario: Team dropdown lists only Teams the user belongs to
Pre-conditions: TeamsMembershipResolver returns 3 Teams for current user
Test Steps: 1. Open scr_CreateTracker; 2. Expand ddTeam
Expected Result: ddTeam shows exactly the 3 Teams from colMyTeams (MicrosoftTeams.GetAllTeams()); no extra Teams visible

Test Scenario: Channel dropdown cascades from selected Team
Pre-conditions: Team has 7 channels the user can read
Test Steps: 1. Select Team in ddTeam; 2. Expand ddChannel
Expected Result: ddChannel.Items resolves to MicrosoftTeams.GetChannelsForGroup(ddTeam.Selected.id).value; only readable channels are listed

Test Scenario: Teams Channel Link auto-derived and read-only
Test Steps: 1. Select Team and Channel; 2. Inspect txtChannelLink
Expected Result: txtChannelLink.Default = ddChannel.Selected.webUrl; control DisplayMode = View; value contains https://teams.microsoft.com/l/channel/...

Test Scenario: Cancel button returns to Landing without saving
Test Steps: 1. Enter OppId 123456; 2. Tap btnCancel
Expected Result: Navigate(scr_Landing); no Patch executed; no row written to gt_graphictracker

Test Scenario: OppId less than 6 digits rejected
Test Steps: 1. Enter OppId 12345; 2. Tap btnCreate
Expected Result: IsMatch fails ^\d{6}$; Notify error "Opportunity ID must be 6 digits"; Patch not invoked

Test Scenario: OppId greater than 6 digits rejected
Test Steps: 1. Enter OppId 1234567; 2. Tap btnCreate
Expected Result: Same validation message; Patch not invoked

Test Scenario: OppId with non-numeric characters rejected
Test Steps: 1. Enter OppId AB1234; 2. Tap btnCreate
Expected Result: Validation error surfaces; Patch not invoked

Test Scenario: Duplicate Opportunity ID rejected by Dataverse alt-key
Test Steps: 1. Open scr_CreateTracker; 2. Enter OppId 123456; 3. Complete fields; 4. Tap btnCreate
Expected Result: Patch returns error; IsBlankOrError(gblActiveTracker) true; Notify "Opportunity ID already exists"; no duplicate row created

Test Scenario: Empty Deal Name rejected
Test Steps: 1. Enter OppId 123456; 2. Leave Deal Name empty; 3. Select Team/Channel; 4. Tap btnCreate
Expected Result: Notify "All fields are required"; Patch not invoked

Test Scenario: Channel dropdown disabled until Team selected
Test Steps: 1. Open scr_CreateTracker; 2. Attempt to expand ddChannel without selecting Team
Expected Result: ddChannel.Items returns blank or control is disabled until ddTeam.Selected is non-blank

Test Scenario: Gallery shows only trackers user has access to
Pre-conditions: User is member of 2 of 10 Teams owning trackers
Test Steps: 1. Open scr_OpenTracker; 2. Observe galTrackers
Expected Result: Filter(gt_graphictrackers, gt_teamid in gblMyTeamIds) returns 2 rows; only those trackers render in gallery

Test Scenario: Search filters within 500ms of keystroke
Pre-conditions: User has access to 5 trackers including "Acme RFP"
Test Steps: 1. Type "Acme" in txtSearch; 2. Observe galTrackers
Expected Result: Gallery filters by StartsWith(gt_opportunityid, txtSearch.Text) or gt_dealname in txtSearch.Text; refresh under 500ms

Test Scenario: Sort dropdown reorders gallery without losing scroll
Test Steps: 1. Select "Deal Name" in ddSort; 2. Scroll mid-gallery; 3. Select "Opportunity ID"
Expected Result: SortByColumns reorders galTrackers; visible scroll position preserved across re-sort

Test Scenario: Row click sets gblActiveTracker and navigates to scr_ViewTracker
Pre-conditions: At least one tracker visible in galTrackers
Test Steps: 1. Tap a tracker row
Expected Result: Set(gblActiveTracker, ThisItem) executes; Navigate(scr_ViewTracker, ScreenTransition.None) fires

Test Scenario: User with no Team memberships sees empty state
Test Steps: 1. Open scr_OpenTracker
Expected Result: galTrackers renders no rows; empty-state "No trackers available" label is visible

Test Scenario: Search with no matches shows no-results message
Pre-conditions: User has accessible trackers but none match search
Test Steps: 1. Type "ZZZZZ" in txtSearch
Expected Result: Gallery is empty; empty-state "No trackers match your search" shown; no error toast

Test Scenario: All graphic requests for active tracker displayed
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to scr_ViewTracker; 2. Observe galRequests
Expected Result: Filter(gt_graphicrequests, gt_trackerid.gt_trackerid = gblActiveTracker.gt_trackerid) returns 5 rows; all render with Control Number, Title, Status badge, Type, Designer

Test Scenario: 30s auto-refresh shows new item after AutonumberFlow completes
Test Steps: 1. Stay on scr_ViewTracker; 2. From another session submit a new item via scr_NewItem; 3. Wait for tmrAutoRefresh
Expected Result: Within 30s, tmrAutoRefresh.OnTimerEnd executes Refresh(gt_graphicrequests); new row with stamped GraphicControlNumber appears in galRequests

Test Scenario: Group-by and Sort-by re-render gallery
Pre-conditions: Tracker has 10 items with varied Status/Designer/Type
Test Steps: 1. Select "Status" in ddGroup; 2. Select "Created Date" in ddSort
Expected Result: GroupBy + SortByColumns re-render gallery into Status buckets; each bucket sorted by createdon descending

Test Scenario: Status badge colors render correctly per status
Pre-conditions: Tracker has rows in all 4 status values
Test Steps: 1. Open scr_ViewTracker; 2. Inspect lblStatusBadge.Fill for each row
Expected Result: New=RGBA(180,180,180,1); InProgress=RGBA(176,141,58,1); UpdatesNeeded=RGBA(184,52,26,1); Completed=RGBA(27,94,32,1)

Test Scenario: + New Item button navigates to scr_NewItem with active tracker context
Test Steps: 1. Tap btnNewItem
Expected Result: Navigate(scr_NewItem, ScreenTransition.None); gblActiveTracker remains in context

Test Scenario: Control-number-pending banner shows when stamp delayed > 60s
Pre-conditions: Row exists with gt_graphiccontrolnumber blank or "PENDING"
Test Steps: 1. Force AutonumberFlow off in DEV; 2. Submit new item; 3. Wait 60s
Expected Result: pendingBanner.Visible=true; banner text "Control number pending — will appear automatically"; banner clears on next refresh once stamp arrives

Test Scenario: Submit valid request creates row and triggers AutonumberFlow
Pre-conditions: User on scr_NewItem; gblActiveTracker set for OppId 123456 / 2026
Test Steps: 1. Fill Title, Type, Instructions, Section, Author, Designer; 2. Leave Email-to-Author ON; 3. Tap btnSave
Expected Result: Patch writes gt_graphicrequest with gt_status=New; AutonumberFlow polls within ~5s and stamps GraphicControlNumber 123456.26.0001

Test Scenario: Submitted By auto-fills from gblUser
Test Steps: 1. Open scr_NewItem; 2. Inspect lblSubmittedBy
Expected Result: lblSubmittedBy reads gblUser.displayName; control DisplayMode = View

Test Scenario: Status defaults to New and not editable on create
Test Steps: 1. Open scr_NewItem; 2. Inspect Status control
Expected Result: Status field shows "New"; control is disabled or absent on create form; Patch sets gt_status: 'gt_status'.New

Test Scenario: Email-to-Author toggle defaults ON
Test Steps: 1. Open scr_NewItem; 2. Inspect tglEmailAuthor
Expected Result: tglEmailAuthor.Value=true on load; user can toggle OFF; Patch persists tglEmailAuthor.Value into gt_emailstatusupdatestoauthor

Test Scenario: Control number appears via 3s polling timer
Test Steps: 1. Save row; 2. Stay on scr_ViewTracker; 3. Observe row
Expected Result: gblNewRequest.gt_graphiccontrolnumber populates within ~30s in format ^\d{6}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$; tmrPollControl.Start set false on success

Test Scenario: btnSave disabled when Title is empty
Test Steps: 1. Leave txtTitle empty; 2. Fill ddType; 3. Inspect btnSave
Expected Result: btnSave.DisplayMode = Disabled; on click Notify "Title and Graphics Type are required"

Test Scenario: btnSave disabled when Graphics Type is empty
Test Steps: 1. Fill txtTitle; 2. Leave ddType empty; 3. Inspect btnSave
Expected Result: btnSave.DisplayMode = Disabled; on click Notify validation error

Test Scenario: Direct attempt to set Status on create is ignored
Pre-conditions: User on scr_NewItem; binding hack attempted
Test Steps: 1. Attempt to bind ddStatus or PATCH with non-default value
Expected Result: Patch sets gt_status to New regardless; submitted value ignored

Test Scenario: Update Status to Updates Needed saves and triggers StatusChangeFlow
Test Steps: 1. Open item in scr_EditItem; 2. Select ddStatus = "Updates Needed"; 3. Tap btnSave
Expected Result: Patch updates gt_status; StatusChangeFlow fires; "Updates Needed for Graphics" email arrives at Designer within 30s

Test Scenario: Source File Control Number matching regex accepted
Test Steps: 1. Enter Source File 123456.26.0042 in txtSourceFileCtrl; 2. Tap btnSave
Expected Result: IsMatch ^\d{6}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$ passes; Patch persists gt_sourcefilecontrolnumber

Test Scenario: Graphic Control Number rendered read-only with click-to-copy
Pre-conditions: Existing item with stamped Control Number
Test Steps: 1. Open scr_EditItem; 2. Attempt to focus lblControlNumber; 3. Tap lblControlNumber
Expected Result: DisplayMode = View; Copy(gt_graphiccontrolnumber) puts value on clipboard; Notify "Control number copied"

Test Scenario: Editable fields persist on save
Test Steps: 1. Edit Title, Type, Instructions, Section, Author, Designer, Email-to-Author; 2. Tap btnSave
Expected Result: Patch persists all editable fields; Back() returns to scr_ViewTracker; refreshed row shows updated values

Test Scenario: Source File Control Number violating regex rejected
Test Steps: 1. Enter "abc.xy.zzzz" in txtSourceFileCtrl; 2. Tap btnSave
Expected Result: varSrcError=true; Notify "Source File Control Number format must be NNNNNN.YY.NNNN"; Patch not invoked

Test Scenario: Direct PATCH of gt_graphiccontrolnumber rejected
Pre-conditions: Existing item with stamped Control Number
Test Steps: 1. Using Dataverse Web API send PATCH on gt_graphicrequest({id}) with new gt_graphiccontrolnumber
Expected Result: Plugin throws InvalidPluginExecutionException "GraphicControlNumber is read-only after generation"; HTTP 400; row unchanged

Test Scenario: New row create triggers atomic increment and stamp
Pre-conditions: AutonumberFlow enabled; gt_ordersequence row for (123456,26) absent
Test Steps: 1. Insert new gt_graphicrequest row for tracker OppId 123456, Year 2026; 2. Wait for flow run; 3. Inspect run history
Expected Result: Flow adds OrderSequence row with gt_lastorder=1; PATCH stamps gt_graphiccontrolnumber=123456.26.0001 on originating row; New-Graphics email sent

Test Scenario: Sequential submissions increment correctly
Test Steps: 1. Submit 3 new items sequentially
Expected Result: Control numbers stamped 123456.26.0006, 0007, 0008; gt_ordersequence.gt_lastorder=8

Test Scenario: Independent sequences per (OppId
Test Steps: Two OrderSequence rows exist
Test Data: 1. Submit item for OppId 123456 / 2026; 2. Submit item for OppId 654321 / 2026; 3. Submit item for OppId 123456 / 2027
Expected Result: Mixed OppId/Year

Test Scenario: Concurrent inserts yield unique consecutive numbers via ETag retry
Pre-conditions: AutonumberFlow enabled; ETag concurrency configured (5 jittered retries on 412)
Test Steps: 1. From PowerShell driver POST 10 parallel rows for same (OppId,Year); 2. Inspect rows after flow runs
Expected Result: All 10 rows receive unique consecutive Control Numbers (0001-0010); flow run history shows 412/retry pairs; no duplicates; no gaps

Test Scenario: New-Graphics email sent to Designer with correct fields
Pre-conditions: Designer email configured on row; Outlook connector healthy
Test Steps: 1. Submit a new item; 2. Inspect Designer Outlook inbox
Expected Result: Email received; subject "New Graphics for Proposal - [Opportunity Name]"; HTML body table includes Control Number, Title, Submitter, Designer

Test Scenario: Update event does not re-fire AutonumberFlow
Pre-conditions: Existing item with stamped Control Number
Test Steps: 1. Edit Title on existing row; 2. Save; 3. Inspect flow run history
Expected Result: AutonumberFlow trigger filter "row added" does not match; no run; stamped Control Number unchanged

Test Scenario: High-water alert at gt_lastorder >= 9000
Test Steps: 1. Submit new item; 2. Wait for scheduled flow run (daily 06:00 UTC) or run on-demand
Expected Result: flow_OrderSequenceHighWater lists rows with gt_lastorder>=9000 and emails btlead@accenture.com with table of OppId/Year/LastOrder

Test Scenario: Platform outage — row persists and stamps on recovery
Test Steps: 1. Disable flow; 2. Submit new item; 3. Verify "Control number pending" banner; 4. Re-enable flow; 5. Verify resume run
Expected Result: Row persists with blank Control Number; pendingBanner visible; on flow re-enable, resume / catch-up run stamps Control Number; banner clears on next 30s refresh

Test Scenario: InProgress → UpdatesNeeded sends email to Designer
Test Steps: 1. Set gt_status=UpdatesNeeded via scr_EditItem; 2. Save; 3. Inspect Designer inbox
Expected Result: Flow Switch case 3 fires; renderUpdatesNeededEmail dispatched; subject "Updates Needed for Graphics - [Opportunity Name]"

Test Scenario: InProgress → Completed sends email to Submitter and CCs Author when toggle ON
Test Steps: 1. Set gt_status=Completed; 2. Save; 3. Inspect Submitter + Author inboxes
Expected Result: Flow Switch case 4 fires; Send an email (V2) To: submitter, Cc: author; subject "Graphics Completed for Proposal - [Opportunity Name]"

Test Scenario: Toggle OFF excludes Author CC on Completed
Test Steps: 1. Trigger status to Completed; 2. Inspect Author inbox
Expected Result: Cc expression evaluates to null; Author does not receive a copy; Submitter still receives the email

Test Scenario: Saving without status change does not run flow
Test Steps: 1. Edit Title only; 2. Save; 3. Inspect run history
Expected Result: Trigger condition (gt_status change) not met; flow does not run; no email

Test Scenario: Downward transition routes to no-email default branch
Test Steps: 1. Set gt_status=New; 2. Save
Expected Result: Switch default branch executes terminate(Succeeded); no email dispatched

Test Scenario: Two-user happy path within Power Apps Player
Pre-conditions: Two UAT users (Submitter and Designer) signed-in to Power Apps Player with GraphicTrackerUser role
Test Steps: 1. Submitter launches app; 2. Creates tracker; 3. Adds new item; 4. AutonumberFlow stamps; 5. Designer receives email; 6. Designer launches app; 7. Opens item via scr_OpenTracker → scr_EditItem; 8. Sets Status=Completed; 9. Submitter receives email
Expected Result: Each hop verifiable in Power Automate Run History and Dataverse audit log; all three emails delivered; row-level security honoured across both users

Test Scenario: Outage simulation: row persists
Test Steps: SIT
Expected Result: 1. Disable AutonumberFlow; 2. Submit new item via scr_NewItem; 3. Wait 60s; 4. Re-enable flow; 5. Wait 30s
column14: Step 3: row persists with blank Control Number and pendingBanner visible. Step 5: catch-up flow run stamps Control Number; pendingBanner clears; gallery shows stamped value
